title: SEC4: notify callback JS exception silently swallowed
description: transport.rs line 113-118: the notification dispatch path calls f.call1(...) and maps the result with .map() but discards it. A JS exception thrown inside the callback is silently dropped — call1 returns Result<JsValue, JsValue> and the outer .map() just discards Ok/Err. There is no comment explaining this is intentional. A maintainer debugging a callback that throws will see no error. Fix: add comment // JS exceptions in the notify callback are intentionally ignored — the relay loop must not crash on bad user-supplied handlers, or propagate errors to console.error.
close_reason: duplicate of nie-7bv6.5: both report the silent discard of f.call1() result in the notify callback path on transport.rs lines 113-118
